title: El Niño is coming. Here is where the risks to agriculture are highest
content: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.
snippet: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.

title: Understanding the global landscape of heat early warning systems
content: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.
snippet: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.

title: HERA launches the world’s first heat-pregnancy insurance to protect expectant mothers from extreme heat
content: HERA and its partners launched HERA Materna, the world’s first ever heat-pregnancy insurance program. Extreme heat is threatening pregnant women on the frontlines of the climate crisis, and until now no financial protection has existed to shield them.
snippet: HERA and its partners launched HERA Materna, the world’s first ever heat-pregnancy insurance program. Extreme heat is threatening pregnant women on the frontlines of the climate crisis, and until now

title: Increase in wildfire-driven ozone linked to premature deaths across the U.S.
content: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.
snippet: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.

title: Heat waves can leave homes dangerously hot – even for young, healthy adults
content: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.
snippet: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.

title: Chronic heat: impacts and solutions from South-East Asia and East Africa
content: While populations in continental regions are predominantly exposed to seasonal cumulative excess heat, many populations in tropical and subtropical climates are chronically exposed to high temperatures and high humidity.

title: World Risk Poll 2026 report: Alone together: The hidden consensus on climate change
content: This report explores public perceptions of the threat of climate change - perceptions that are shaped by people’s lived experience of risk in their daily lives as much as by scientific evidence.
snippet: This report explores public perceptions of the threat of climate change - perceptions that are shaped by people’s lived experience of risk in their daily lives as much as by scientific evidence.

title: Tracking the long journey of smoke from wildfires
content: When we see a forest fire on the news, it is easy to think of it as a local emergency. A new CMCC study shows that smoke from Italian forest fires can travel hundreds to even thousands of kilometers, carrying fine particles.
snippet: When we see a forest fire on the news, it is easy to think of it as a local emergency. A new CMCC study shows that smoke from Italian forest fires can travel hundreds to even thousands of kilometers,

title: Unpacking disaster risk reduction (DRR): Building practical approaches to identify and reduce risks
content: AIDR is offering this workshop for anyone who has a role in reducing risk, building resilience, or wants to take action in their work or community.
snippet: AIDR is offering this workshop for anyone who has a role in reducing risk, building resilience, or wants to take action in their work or community.

title: Disaster recovery for all
content: AIDR is offering this workshop for delegates seeking a deeper exploration of disaster recovery. We welcome all interested delegates, with anticipated interest from all levels of government, community organisations, agencies and researchers.

title: World (MDPI)
content: World is an international, peer-reviewed , open access journal on past, present, and future links between economic, political, social, and/or environmental issues, published monthly online by MDPI.
snippet: World is an international, peer-reviewed , open access journal on past, present, and future links between economic, political, social, and/or environmental issues, published monthly online by MDPI.

title: Geographies (MDPI)
content: Geographies is an international, peer-reviewed, open access journal on geography published quarterly online by MDPI.
snippet: Geographies is an international, peer-reviewed, open access journal on geography published quarterly online by MDPI.

title: Journal of Coastal Research
content: Journal of Coastal Research (JCR) publishes content relevant to natural and engineered coastline environments (freshwater, brackish, or marine) and the protection/management of their resources.
snippet: (JCR) publishes content relevant to natural and engineered coastline environments (freshwater, brackish, or marine) and the protection/management of their resources.

title: Belize shows why investing in early warnings pays off
content: In Belize, the value of early warnings is now being shown not only through the lives and livelihoods they help protect, but also through the economic benefits they bring.
snippet: In Belize, the value of early warnings is now being shown not only through the lives and livelihoods they help protect, but also through the economic benefits they bring.

title: Homa Bay approves people-led climate adaptation plan
content: Across Africa, the impacts of climate change are often felt first and most severely by low-income communities living in informal settlements.
snippet: Across Africa, the impacts of climate change are often felt first and most severely by low-income communities living in informal settlements.

title: Technical Analyst, Geospatial AI

title: The surprising hurdle holding back climate action
content: For the first time, the World Risk Poll has measured not just how people around the world view the threat of climate change, but also whether they believe their fellow citizens feel the same.
snippet: For the first time, the World Risk Poll has measured not just how people around the world view the threat of climate change, but also whether they believe their fellow citizens feel the same.

title: Kenya disaster risk financing strategy 2026 - 2030
content: This strategy aims to strengthen national and county governments’ financial capacity to manage disaster risks across the DRM continuum, protect vulnerable groups, safeguard development gains, build resilience, and maintain fiscal stability.

title: The quiet hazards: How everyday risk shapes daily life
content: Even as global rates of workplace injury are declining, action is still needed to reduce harm amongst the most vulnerable. There are also signs of complacency in the face of rising harm from food, drinking water and poor air quality.

title: As the world warms, the risk of snakebites is rising
content: Climate change is increasing human-snake encounters, even as many countries remain ill-equipped to treat victims
snippet: Climate change is increasing human-snake encounters, even as many countries remain ill-equipped to treat victims

title: Analysing wildfire behaviour can help detect risk zones earlier and support fire‑smart strategies
content: Fire-smart risk assessment is needed to tackle the scale of wildfire destruction, which is a growing reality across the globe. Hazardous fires are more intense and more frequent, fuelled both by climate change and human footprint.

title: Tennis players and fans are facing more extreme weather at the four Grand Slams
content: Sporting events around the world are being impacted by extreme weather, including heat and heavy rainfall, and tennis is no exception.
snippet: Sporting events around the world are being impacted by extreme weather, including heat and heavy rainfall, and tennis is no exception.

title: Asymmetric economic impacts of climate extremes on Italian agriculture: a nonlinear ARDL approach
content: This study investigates the asymmetric economic impacts of climate extremes on the Italian agricultural sector.
snippet: This study investigates the asymmetric economic impacts of climate extremes on the Italian agricultural sector.

title: Country perspectives on pre-arranged financing: Belize, Grenada and Jamaica
content: This report presents a comparative synthesis of how stakeholders in three Caribbean Small Island Developing States — Belize, Grenada, and Jamaica — value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing (PAF) for sovereign disaster risk.
snippet: This report presents a comparative synthesis of how stakeholders in three Caribbean Small Island Developing States — Belize, Grenada, and Jamaica — value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing

title: Country perspectives on pre-arranged financing: Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, Senegal and Zambia
content: This report presents the first cross-country study to use a structured simulation to elicit how policymakers in five African countries value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing (PAF) systems for disaster response.
snippet: This report presents the first cross-country study to use a structured simulation to elicit how policymakers in five African countries value the core attributes of pre-arranged financing (PAF) systems

content: This brief reviews the empirical evidence on the barriers women face in adapting to climate change in rural Sub-Saharan Africa. A

title: What governments want from pre-arranged financing: evidence from Africa and the Caribbean
content: This policy brief presents the first cross-country study of what governments want from pre-arranged financing (PAF) — funding secured in advance to respond to disasters.
snippet: This policy brief presents the first cross-country study of what governments want from pre-arranged financing (PAF) — funding secured in advance to respond to disasters.

title: Where heat does not come in waves: a framework for understanding and managing chronic heat
content: This study argues that heat research and practice have focused too narrowly on heat waves as acute, episodic emergencies, obscuring the chronic exposure of billions of people to dangerous levels of heat for months at a time.

title: HLPF session: Protection of persons in the event of disasters: does the proposed agreement reflect ground realities?
content: This session aims to examine the draft agreement on "Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED) in the context of ground realities and 2030 agenda.
snippet: This session aims to examine the draft agreement on "Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED) in the context of ground realities and 2030 agenda.

title: Competing in the face of climate risks: evidence from firms and policy priorities in MENAAP
content: This World Bank Development Report examines how rising climate risks are reshaping the competitiveness of firms across the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan, and Pakistan (MENAAP) region.
snippet: This World Bank Development Report examines how rising climate risks are reshaping the competitiveness of firms across the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan, and Pakistan (MENAAP) region.

title: Eleven lessons in risk governance from the Panama Canal
content: In more than a century of operation, the authorities that manage the Panama Canal have accumulated some valuable lessons in risk governance that are relevant to other major infrastructure systems – and to disaster risk reduction more broadly.

title: Delivering a 'double dividend' for MENAAP firms in extreme heat – World Bank report
content: The flagship report presents cross-country, firm-level evidence on the tangible impact rising temperatures and climate-related trade policies have on financial markets.
snippet: The flagship report presents cross-country, firm-level evidence on the tangible impact rising temperatures and climate-related trade policies have on financial markets.

title: How FIFA’s climate solution has turned into ‘water-gate’
content: Introduced in the name of player safety, hydration breaks at the World Cup have become a flashpoint for fans and players alike.
snippet: Introduced in the name of player safety, hydration breaks at the World Cup have become a flashpoint for fans and players alike.

title: A review of published IDF parametric case studies: Findings and takeaways
content: This paper provides a summary review of the collection of parametric case studies. The paper highlights key takeaways from the case studies, including how solutions are already delivering rapid liquidity after disasters, & closing critical protection gaps

title: ITALERT: Assessing the quality of LLMs and NMT in translating Italian emergency response text
content: This paper presents the outcomes of an initial investigation into the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Neural Machine Translation (NMT) systems in translating high-stakes messages.
snippet: This paper presents the outcomes of an initial investigation into the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Neural Machine Translation (NMT) systems in translating high-stakes messages.

title: Climate change adaptations for WASH
content: This guidance note provides a practical framework for integrating climate change adaptation into water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) programming in humanitarian settings.
snippet: This guidance note provides a practical framework for integrating climate change adaptation into water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) programming in humanitarian settings.

title: The tech that warns millions in seconds and the risk that comes with it
content: Experts say when people receive too many warnings, especially for events that do not directly affect them, they begin to tune them out.
snippet: Experts say when people receive too many warnings, especially for events that do not directly affect them, they begin to tune them out.

title: ACL Anthology
content: The ACL Anthology is a library of publications in the scientific fields of computational linguistics and speech and natural language processing.
snippet: The ACL Anthology is a library of publications in the scientific fields of computational linguistics and speech and natural language processing.

title: Competing in the face of climate risks: Evidence from firms and policy priorities in MENAAP
content: This report focuses on two concrete and immediate examples of how heat- and trade-related policies in destination markets affect firms and the broader economy of the MENAAP region.
snippet: This report focuses on two concrete and immediate examples of how heat- and trade-related policies in destination markets affect firms and the broader economy of the MENAAP region.

title: Investigating the deployment of Takaful for farmers in flood and drought-prone regions in Nigeria
content: This study has investigated the deployment of takaful for farmers in flood- and drought-prone regions in Nigeria. The study used a quantitative research design with cross-sectional survey data from smallholder farmers in some climate-prone zones

title: Towards an operational European Drought Impacts Database (EDID)
content: This article gives insight into the implementation of European Drought Impact Database (EDID), its structure and attributes, and provides an analysis of the content.
snippet: This article gives insight into the implementation of European Drought Impact Database (EDID), its structure and attributes, and provides an analysis of the content.

title: El Niño impacts on food security: Lessons from past El Niño events for 2026/27 preparedness (June 2026)
content: The study examines the main impacts of the 2015/16 and 2023/24 El Niño events on drought, flooding, displacement, and the agriculture sector.
snippet: The study examines the main impacts of the 2015/16 and 2023/24 El Niño events on drought, flooding, displacement, and the agriculture sector.
